steps.title: Find the context clues that define the phrase
steps.content: The phrase "I couldn't be stopped" appears in the context of the author talking. The very next sentences explain why: her sister's friends "could hardly stand to ride to school" with her, and she was "loud and unrelenting." The word "unrelenting" — meaning nonstop and persistent — directly explains what "couldn't be stopped" means: she kept talking without stopping.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — She had a hard time sitting still.: The passage is about her talking, not physical restlessness. Nothing in the text connects "couldn't be stopped" to movement. ✗
  • Choice B — She often changed her mind.: Changing one's mind is unrelated to being loud and unrelenting. The text gives no evidence of fickleness. ✗
  • Choice C — She was well liked as a child.: The opposite is implied: her sister's friends "could hardly stand" to ride with her because of her excessive talking. ✗
  • Choice D — She seemed to talk all the time.: This directly matches "unrelenting" — she kept talking nonstop, which is why others could barely tolerate being around her. ✓
That matches Choice D — She seemed to talk all the time..
